texto: If the universe is infinite, then every possibility must exist in reality, yes?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Zachary Rawlins que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “If the universe is infinite, then every possibility must exist in reality, yes?”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, universe, infinite, com foco em reflexao.

frase_original: If the universe is infinite, then every possibility must exist in reality, yes?

texto: Its complicated, on one level. On another, its the same old stupid story - we aren't enlightened. We disagree, fall in love, and hate eachother, the whole spectrum of human experience. We have differences of opinion, and sometimes, we can't resolve those differences peacefully. If a disagreement goes for long enough, and is important enough, people start to take sides. Once people start to take sides, conflict is inevitable.
explicacao: Citação de Zachary Rawlins sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Its complicated, on one level. On another, its the same old stupid story - we aren't enlightened.…” articula amor, conflict, human-nature em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como human-nature e love.

We disagree, fall in love, and hate eachother, the whole spectrum of human experience. We have differences of opinion, and sometimes, we can't resolve those differences peacefully. If a disagreement goes for long enough, and is important enough, people start to take sides. Once people start to take sides, conflict is inevitable.
